_meta.author: Claude (national autopilot batch)
_meta.note: GA state legislature batch SD31/HD32/SD32/HD33/SD33 (state tier). Evidence from GA party-line marquee votes (SB202 election integrity, SB319 constitutional carry, SB140 transgender-minors ban, HB1105 immigration, SB36 RFRA — all recorded party-line), TPAction GA scorecard roll calls, iVoterGuide self-statements, NFIB/ACU/Faith&Freedom/GA-Chamber ratings, NRA/Georgia Life Alliance endorsements. TWO high-value catches: (1) Kay Kirkpatrick (R-SD32), a physician, deviates on life — stated she would have voted AGAINST the 2019 heartbeat bill and backs rape/incest/life exceptions (sanctity_of_life FALSE) despite a GLA endorsement; (2) Alan Powell (R-HD33), Regulated Industries chair, is GA's leading champion of casino/sports-betting/gambling expansion (industry_capture FALSE), and is now in a 2026 redistricting primary against fellow incumbent Rob Leverett. Michael Rhett (D-SD33) scored FALSE only on verified party-line marquee votes; cells with no cited record left null (freedomindex.us direction data found unreliable and not used).

profile.background: GA State Senator, District 31 (R) since Jan 2021 (Paulding/Polk). Elected Senate Majority Leader June 2025. Former Doraville councilman and Paulding County school board member; primary author of Georgia's constitutional-carry law (SB319).

jason-anavitarte@GA.notes_set: GA State Senator, SD-31 (R), since 2021; Senate Majority Leader (since June 2025). [2026-06 national refinement, state tier] Uniformly conservative leadership record: primary author of SB319 constitutional carry (2022); cosponsor of SB202 Election Integrity Act (2021); backed SB233 Georgia Promise Scholarship ESA (2024); listed supporter of SB140 (ban on gender-transition procedures for minors, 2023); introduced 2024 illegal-immigration bail/enforcement legislation; NFIB-GA 100%, Faith & Freedom Coalition GA 102%; 'I will never support a tax increase.' No cross-party deviations found. Dimensions without a cited individual record left null.

0.note: States life is protected conception-to-natural-death and supports embryo protection; Faith & Freedom Coalition GA 102% rating.

2.note: Listed among supporters of SB140 (ban on gender-transition surgery/hormones for minors, 2023).

0.note: Senate GOP leadership backed SB233 Georgia Promise Scholarship (ESA voucher), enacted 2024.

2.note: NFIB-GA 100%; 'I will never support a tax increase'; authored SB47 firearm sales-tax holiday.

1.note: Cosponsor of SB202, the 2021 Election Integrity Act (voter-ID/election security).
Introduced 2024 legislation denying bail/parole to removable aliens (immigration enforcement).
Primary author of SB319 constitutional carry, signed 2022 (GA = 25th constitutional-carry state).
2024 bill sets stricter bail criteria for removable-alien/felony defendants (tough-on-crime).

profile.religion: United Methodist — Homer United Methodist Church (lay leader)
profile.background: GA State Representative, District 32 (R) since 2019 (Banks/Habersham, NE Georgia); Chairman, House Education Committee. Former Banks County school superintendent (11 yrs) and 2013 Georgia Superintendent of the Year.

chris-erwin@GA.notes_set: GA State Rep, HD-32 (R), since 2019; House Education Committee chair. [2026-06 national refinement, state tier] Consistent conservative record (Turning Point Action GA 94.6 lifetime, 100 in 2026): YES on SB36 Georgia RFRA (2025, party-line); YES on SB1 (2025 'Fair & Safe Athletic Opportunities Act' barring males from girls' sports); as Education chair advanced SB233 ESA (2024) plus SB212/HB268; YES on HB1001 income-tax cut (2026, GA Chamber A+); YES on HB963 election-security (2026); YES on HB1076 (felony vehicular obstruction of an officer). No cross-party deviation. No individual 2A roll call or pro-life-group letter grade isolated — those dimensions kept null/soft.

1.note: Member of the post-LIFE-Act GA GOP caucus; YES on HB350 (2026 newborn safe-haven). Turning Point Action GA 94.6 lifetime.

2.note: YES on SB1 (2025) barring males from girls' sports — rejects gender-identity mandate.

0.note: As House Education chair advanced SB233 ESA (Georgia Promise Scholarship, signed 2024); YES on SB212/HB268.

1.note: YES on SB36, the 2025 Georgia Religious Freedom Restoration Act (party-line).

2.note: YES on HB1001 (2026 income-tax reduction); Georgia Chamber A+.

1.note: YES on HB963 (2026) barring foreign nationals / election security.
YES on HB1076 (felony vehicular obstruction of a law-enforcement officer) and SB185 (2025).

profile.religion: United Methodist — East Cobb United Methodist Church
profile.background: GA State Senator, District 32 (R) since 2017 (east Cobb County). Retired orthopaedic hand surgeon, former president of Resurgens Orthopaedics; first woman elected to SD-32. iVoterGuide rates her overall lean 'Moderate.'

kay-kirkpatrick@GA.notes_set: GA State Senator, SD-32 (R), since 2017. [2026-06 national refinement, state tier] Solidly conservative on guns, economy, immigration, crime and school choice (ACU-GA 96%, NFIB-GA 100%, GA Chamber A+; NRA PVF endorsed; YES SB319 constitutional carry; cosponsor SB140 transgender-minors ban). DOCUMENTED CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION ON LIFE: as a physician she stated she would have voted AGAINST the 2019 HB481 'heartbeat' bill (was excused/no recorded vote) and supports rape/incest/life-of-mother exceptions — sanctity_of_life FALSE — despite a Georgia Life Alliance endorsement. Stance-based cells from her iVoterGuide questionnaire are scored soft.

1.note: DEVIATION: a physician who stated she would have voted AGAINST the 2019 HB481 heartbeat bill and supports rape/incest/life-of-mother exceptions (iVoterGuide), short of personhood-from-conception — despite a GLA endorsement.

2.note: Cosponsor of SB140 (2023) banning gender-transition care for minors; disagrees gender identity should be a protected class.

0.note: Supports school choice — vouchers, tax credits, charter schools (own iVoterGuide statement); chairs the Children & Families Committee.

1.note: States businesses should not be forced to provide services that violate their religious beliefs (conscience protection).

2.note: NFIB-GA 100%, GA Chamber A+, ACU-GA 96%; favors flatter tax code / zero-based budgeting.

1.note: On the GOP recorded YES side of the party-line SB202 (34-20) Election Integrity Act (2021).
States the U.S. should do more to physically secure the southern border (own statement).
YES on SB319 constitutional carry (2022); endorsed by NRA PVF.

0.note: Supports police qualified immunity and strongly opposes defunding the police (own statement).

profile.background: GA State Representative, District 33 (R) since 1991 (Hartwell, NE Georgia); originally a Democrat, switched to Republican in 2010. Chairs the House Regulated Industries Committee (gambling/alcohol portfolio); long service on Public Safety/Homeland Security. Due to 2026 redistricting he faces fellow incumbent Rob Leverett (R) in the May 19, 2026 GOP primary for HD-33.

alan-powell@GA.notes_set: GA State Rep, HD-33 (R), since 1991 (former Democrat, switched 2010); Regulated Industries chair. [2026-06 national refinement, state tier] Strongly conservative voting record (Turning Point Action GA 93.5 in 2026, 100 lifetime key issues): YES on SB36 Georgia RFRA (2025, party-line); YES on school-choice SB1/SB233/HB268/SB212; YES on HB1001 tax cut (NFIB-GA 100%, ACU-GA 90%); defended the 2021 Election Integrity Act and YES on HB963 (2026); strong 2A record with NRA-ILA recognition; YES on HB947/SB185 medical-freedom bills. CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION: as Regulated Industries chair he is Georgia's leading legislative champion of expanding legalized gambling (casinos, sports betting, horse racing) — industry_capture FALSE. CURRENCY NOTE: redistricting pits him against incumbent Rob Leverett (R) in the 2026 HD-33 primary — verify which incumbent survives. No church/religion documented.

0.note: Long pro-life reputation; Faith & Freedom Coalition GA 75% / ACU-GA 90% (issue-group ratings). No completed life questionnaire — soft.
YES on the school-choice slate — SB1/SB233 (Georgia Promise Scholarship ESA)/HB268/SB212.

1.note: YES on SB36, the 2025 Georgia Religious Freedom Restoration Act (House passed 96-70 party-line).
Supported the 2021 Election Integrity Act ('Show me the suppression. There is no suppression in this bill'); YES on HB963 (2026).
Strong 2A record; received NRA-ILA recognition; long service on Public Safety.

0.note: Long Public Safety/Homeland Security service, law-enforcement-aligned; backed 2025 school-safety legislation (soft, committee role).

profile.religion: AME — Turner Chapel AME Church, Marietta (trustee)
profile.background: GA State Senator, District 33 (D) since 2015 (Marietta/Cobb County); first African American elected to the GA Senate from Cobb. Retired Georgia educator (Ed.D., UGA) and retired USAF Reserve Master Sergeant.

michael-rhett@GA.notes_set: GA State Senator, SD-33 (D), since 2015. [2026-06 national refinement, state tier] Standard Democratic voting record; scored ONLY on verified party-line marquee votes (Democrats recorded NO): NO on SB140 (transgender-minors ban), NO on SB202 (Election Integrity Act), NO on SB319 (constitutional carry), and on the party-line side opposing HB1105 (immigration enforcement). No confirmed cross-party deviation toward the conservative position was found — freedomindex.us per-bill direction data for this legislator contradicted verified GA roll calls on every flagship bill and was NOT used. All other dimensions left null (no cited record). Trustee at Turner Chapel AME Church.

2.note: NO on SB140 (party-line; Democrats opposed) limiting gender-transition treatment for minors.

1.note: On the Democratic NO side of the party-line SB202 (34-20) Election Integrity Act (2021).
On the party-line Democratic side opposing HB1105 (2024 immigration-enforcement act); no crossover named.
NO on SB319 constitutional carry (34-22 party-line; Democrats opposed).
